hardware_context_rules.physical_exploitation_vector: Attacker attaches a hardware programmer clip to the External SPI Flash Memory Chip and dumps the binary filesystem logic lanes.
hardware_context_rules.physical_operational_impact: Full system compromise. Attacker reverse-engineers the partition layout, clones the device profile, or flashes arbitrary malicious boot code.
hardware_context_rules.recommended_hardware_mitigation: Transition to a secure microcontroller utilizing Internal Encrypted Flash Memory, or integrate a dedicated Secure Element/TPM chip.

hardware_context_rules.physical_exploitation_vector: Attacker wires a USB-to-UART serial interface converter directly to the exposed UART Serial Test Pads on the board interface.
hardware_context_rules.physical_operational_impact: The attacker accesses an active root command-line terminal during execution, enabling local shell script execution or manual kernel panic overrides.
hardware_context_rules.recommended_hardware_mitigation: Disable production compilation serial console output; omit physical copper testing pads entirely from final production PCB layouts.

hardware_context_rules.physical_exploitation_vector: Attacker connects an external malicious payload device into accessible USB / external expansion bus entry ports.
hardware_context_rules.physical_operational_impact: Irreversible permanent bricking of the operational vehicle asset, or rogue code injection over the localized internal controller network.
hardware_context_rules.recommended_hardware_mitigation: Enforce hardware-rooted Secure Boot routines on the system SoC, requiring cryptographic validation before reading instruction blocks.
